name: Baltanás from Villaconancio – circular via Vertavillo and Cevico de la Torre
name_original: Baltanás desde Villaconancio – circular por Vertavilloy Cevico de la Torre
region_intro: Road cycling around Villaconancio offers routes through the agricultural plains and river valleys of the Palencia province. The terrain is characterized by rolling hills and open landscapes, with elevation gains suitable for various fitness levels. Routes often connect historical villages and pass through areas like the Ribera del Duero, known for its vineyards. This region provides a network of roads for exploring the Castilian countryside. Best road cycling routes around Villaconancio The most popular road cycling route is Monasterio de San Pelayo from Torquemada – circular via Antigüedad and Baltanás, a 65.0 miles (104.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey connecting several towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Baltanás from Villaconancio – circular via Vertavillo and Cevico de la Torre, a moderate 42.2 miles (68.0 km) path. This route provides a circular exploration of the local area, passing through villages. Local road cyclists also love the Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop, a 79.8 miles (128.5 km) trail leading through the Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley, often completed in about 5 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Villaconancio is defined by river valleys, agricultural plains, and historical villages, offering routes for both moderate and difficult skill levels. The routes in Villaconancio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 65 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villaconancio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villaconancio?
A: There are 11 road cycling routes available around Villaconancio, offering a variety of experiences through the agricultural plains and river valleys of the Palencia province.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villaconancio?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy', many of the 8 moderate routes are suitable for cyclists with some experience. These routes feature rolling hills and open landscapes, providing a good challenge without extreme difficulty.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced road cyclists expect in Villaconancio?
A: Advanced road cyclists can find challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169243322/ribera-del-duero-and-esgueva-valley-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 79.8-mile (128.5 km) trail with over 1,100 meters of ascent, offering a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Villaconancio?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159618747/baltanas-from-villaconancio-circular-via-vertavillo-and-cevico-de-la-torre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás from Villaconancio – circular via Vertavillo and Cevico de la Torre&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 42.2-mile (68.0 km) path that explores local villages.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Villaconancio?
A: Road cycling routes often connect historical villages and pass through areas like the Ribera del Duero, known for its vineyards. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5560975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antigüedad&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás Underground Wine Cellars&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in this region?
A: The duration varies depending on the route's length and your pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159862741/monasterio-de-san-pelayo-from-torquemada-circular-via-antigueedad-and-baltanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Pelayo from Torquemada – circular via Antigüedad and Baltanás&lt;/a&gt;, a 65.0-mile (104.6 km) route, typically takes around 4 hours 36 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any less crowded road cycling routes around Villaconancio?
A: The region's routes generally traverse open landscapes and agricultural plains, which tend to be less crowded than urban areas. For a quieter experience, consider routes that venture further into the Castilian countryside, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052794774/tierras-de-el-cerrato-circular-desde-villovela-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierras de El Cerrato — circular desde Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Villaconancio?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the connection to historical villages, and the opportunity to explore the Ribera del Duero region.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Ribera del Duero region?
A: Yes, several routes explore the famous Ribera del Duero region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169243322/ribera-del-duero-and-esgueva-valley-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, offering a long ride through this wine-producing area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villaconancio?
A: The region experiences warm summers and cooler winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with comfortable conditions for longer rides through the open landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, endurance riders can find routes that cover significant distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159537186/palencia-from-quintana-del-puente-circular-via-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-torquemada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia from Quintana del Puente – circular via Castillo de Sarmiento and Torquemada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 152.1-mile (244.8 km) route, providing a substantial challenge for experienced cyclists.
tips: Overlooking the Palencia Cerrato region, the small town of Vertavillo is situated on a significant hill overlooking the surrounding landscape and cereal fields. While cycling through the city, take a look at the stone arch called the Postigo gate.
A nice town to stop for a drink and fill the bottles. The town of Cevico de la Torre is located in the Palencia region of Cerrato.
Baltanás is a small town famous for the underground wine storage cellars that can be found scattered up a hill in the town.
